contentBlocks.original: याज्ञवल्क्यकृत सरस्वती स्तवम्
contentBlocks.transliteration: yājñavalkyakṛta sarasvatī stavam
contentBlocks.translation: Title: The Sarasvatī Praise composed by Yājñavalkya (from the Śrī Devī Bhāgavata Mahāpurāṇa, Ninth Skandha, Fifth Chapter).

contentBlocks.original:
याज्ञवल्क्य उवाच
कृपां कुरु जगन्मातर्‌-मामेवं हततेजसम्‌ ।
गुरुशापात् स्मृतिभ्रष्टं विद्याहीनं च दुःखितम्‌ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yājñavalkya uvācha
kṛpāṃ kuru jaganmātar‌-māmēvaṃ hatatējasam‌ ।
guruśāpāt smṛtibhraṣṭaṃ vidyāhīnaṃ cha duḥkhitam‌ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 1:] Yājñavalkya spoke: O Mother of all worlds — show grace to me who have been thus robbed of my brilliance, who have lost my memory by the guru's curse, who am devoid of learning, and who am sorrowful.
contentBlocks.meanings: yājñavalkya uvācha = Yājñavalkya spoke | kṛpāṃ kuru = show grace | jagat-mātar = O Mother of all worlds | māmēvaṃ = to me in this condition | hata-tējasam = robbed of brilliance/vigour | guru-śāpāt = by the guru's curse | smṛti-bhraṣṭaṃ = fallen from memory | vidyā-hīnaṃ = devoid of learning | cha duḥkhitam = and sorrowful

contentBlocks.original:
ज्ञानं देहि स्मृतिं विद्यां शक्तिं शिष्य प्रबोधिनीम्‌ ।
ग्रन्थकर्तृत्व शक्तिं च सुशिष्यं सुप्रतिष्ठितम्‌ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jñānaṃ dēhi smṛtiṃ vidyāṃ śaktiṃ śiṣya prabōdhinīm‌ ।
granthakartṛtva śaktiṃ cha suśiṣyaṃ supratiṣṭhitam‌ ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 2:] Grant me knowledge, memory, and learning; the power to enlighten disciples; the power of authorship [of great works]; a good and well-established disciple.
contentBlocks.meanings: jñānaṃ dēhi = grant knowledge | smṛtiṃ = memory | vidyāṃ = learning | śaktiṃ śiṣya-prabōdhinīm = the power to enlighten/awaken disciples | grantha-kartṛtva-śaktiṃ = the power of authorship | suśiṣyaṃ = a good disciple | supratiṣṭhitam = well-established

contentBlocks.original:
प्रतिभां सत्सभायां च विचारक्षमतां शुभाम्‌ ।
लुप्तं सर्वं दैव योगा-न्नवीभूतं पुनः कुरु ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pratibhāṃ satsabhāyāṃ cha vichārakṣamatāṃ śubhām‌ ।
luptaṃ sarvaṃ daiva yōgā-nnavībhūtaṃ punaḥ kuru ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 3:] Grant me poetic genius in the assembly of the learned, and the auspicious ability for creative reflection. Make all that was lost by divine ordination fresh and new once again.
contentBlocks.meanings: pratibhāṃ = poetic genius, creative inspiration | satsabhāyāṃ = in the assembly of the good/learned | vichāra-kṣamatāṃ śubhām = the auspicious capacity for reflection | luptaṃ sarvaṃ = all that was lost | daiva-yōgāt = by divine ordination | navī-bhūtaṃ = become new | punaḥ kuru = make once again

contentBlocks.original:
यथाङ्कुरं भस्मनि च करोति देवता पुनः ।
ब्रह्मस्वरूपा परमा ज्योतीरूपा सनातनी ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathāṅkuraṃ bhasmani cha karōti dēvatā punaḥ ।
brahmasvarūpā paramā jyōtīrūpā sanātanī ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 4:] Just as a divine being makes a sprout spring up again even from ashes — O you of the form of Brahman, the Supreme, of the form of light, the eternal one —
contentBlocks.meanings: yathā = just as | aṅkuram = a sprout | bhasmani = in ashes | cha karōti = makes to arise | dēvatā punaḥ = a divine being once again | brahma-svarūpā = O you of the form of Brahman | paramā = the Supreme | jyōtī-rūpā = of the form of light | sanātanī = the eternal one

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वविद्याधिदेवी या तस्यै वाण्यै नमो नमः ।
विसर्ग बिन्दुमात्रासु यदधिष्ठानमेवच ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvavidyādhidēvī yā tasyai vāṇyai namō namaḥ ।
visarga bindumātrāsu yadadhiṣṭhānamēvacha ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 5:] She who is the presiding Goddess of all knowledge — salutation, salutation to that Vāṇī! She who is the very presiding presence in the visarga and bindu marks [of written Sanskrit] —
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-vidyā-adhidēvī = presiding Goddess of all knowledge | yā = she who | tasyai vāṇyai = to that Vāṇī (Sarasvatī) | namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation | visarga = the double-dot mark (:) in Sanskrit | bindu = the nasal dot (ṁ) | mātrāsu = in the marks | yat adhiṣṭhānam = what presiding presence | ēva cha = also

contentBlocks.original:
तदधिष्ठात्री या देवी तस्यै वाण्यै नमो नमः ।
व्याख्यास्वरूपा सा देवी व्याख्याधिष्ठातृरूपिणी ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tadadhiṣṭhātrī yā dēvī tasyai vāṇyai namō namaḥ ।
vyākhyāsvarūpā sā dēvī vyākhyādhiṣṭhātṛrūpiṇī ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 6:] Salutation, salutation to that Vāṇī who is the presiding Goddess there! That Goddess who is the very form of commentary — who takes the form of the presiding deity of all explanation —
contentBlocks.meanings: tat-adhiṣṭhātrī yā dēvī = the Goddess who presides over that | tasyai vāṇyai namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation to that Vāṇī | vyākhyā-svarūpā = she who has the form of commentary/explanation | sā dēvī = that Goddess | vyākhyā-adhiṣṭhātṛ-rūpiṇī = having the form of the presiding deity of commentary

contentBlocks.original:
यया विना प्रसङ्ख्यावान् सङ्ख्यां कर्तुं न शक्यते ।
कालसङ्ख्या स्वरूपा या तस्यै देव्यै नमो नमः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yayā vinā prasaṅkhyāvān saṅkhyāṃ kartuṃ na śakyatē ।
kālasaṅkhyā svarūpā yā tasyai dēvyai namō namaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 7:] She without whom even the most learned mathematician cannot calculate — she who is the very form of temporal reckoning — salutation, salutation to that Goddess!
contentBlocks.meanings: yayā vinā = without whom | prasaṅkhyāvān = even the most learned in reckoning | saṅkhyāṃ kartuṃ = to make calculations | na śakyatē = is not capable | kāla-saṅkhyā-svarūpā = she who has the form of temporal calculation | yā = she who | tasyai dēvyai namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation to that Goddess

contentBlocks.original:
भ्रम सिद्धान्तरूपा या तस्यै देव्यै नमो नमः ।
स्मृतिशक्ति ज्ञानशक्ति बुद्धिशक्ति स्वरूपिणी ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhrama siddhāntarūpā yā tasyai dēvyai namō namaḥ ।
smṛtiśakti jñānaśakti buddhiśakti svarūpiṇī ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 8:] She who is the very form of the resolution of misconceptions — salutation, salutation to that Goddess! She who embodies the powers of memory, knowledge, and intellect —
contentBlocks.meanings: bhrama-siddhānta-rūpā = she of the form of the resolution of misconceptions | yā = she who | tasyai dēvyai namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation to that Goddess | smṛti-śakti = power of memory | jñāna-śakti = power of knowledge | buddhi-śakti = power of intellect | svarūpiṇī = she who has that form

contentBlocks.original:
प्रतिभाकल्पनाशक्तिर्‌-या च तस्यै नमोनमः ।
सनत्कुमारो ब्रह्माणं ज्ञानं पप्रच्छ यत्र वै ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pratibhākalpanāśaktir‌-yā cha tasyai namōnamaḥ ।
sanatkumārō brahmāṇaṃ jñānaṃ paprachCha yatra vai ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 9:] And she who is the power of creative imagination — salutation, salutation to her! [Illustrative story:] When Sanatkumāra once asked Brahmā about knowledge —
contentBlocks.meanings: pratibhā-kalpanā-śaktiḥ = the power of creative imagination/conception | yā cha = and she who | tasyai namō namaḥ = salutation, salutation to her | sanatkumāraḥ = Sanatkumāra (one of the four mind-born sons of Brahmā) | brahmāṇaṃ = Brahmā | jñānaṃ = about knowledge | paprachCha = asked | yatra vai = where indeed

contentBlocks.original:
बभूव मूकवत्सोस्पि सिद्धान्तं कर्तु मक्षमः ।
तदाष्जगाम भगवा-नात्मा श्रीकृष्ण ईश्वरः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
babhūva mūkavatsōspi siddhāntaṃ kartu makṣamaḥ ।
tadāṣjagāma bhagavā-nātmā śrīkṛṣṇa īśvaraḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 10:] [Brahmā] became like a mute, incapable of resolving the matter. Then the Lord Himself — Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Self — came there.
contentBlocks.meanings: babhūva = became | mūka-vat = like a mute | aspi = even he | siddhāntaṃ = the doctrine/resolution | kartum = to make/compose | akṣamaḥ = incapable | tadā = then | ājagāma = came | bhagavān = the Lord | ātmā = the Supreme Self | śrī-kṛṣṇa-īśvaraḥ = Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Lord

contentBlocks.original:
उवाच स च तां स्तौहि वाणी मिष्टां प्रजापते ।
स च तुष्टाव तां ब्रह्मा चाज्ञया परमात्मनः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uvācha sa cha tāṃ stauhi vāṇī miṣṭāṃ prajāpatē ।
sa cha tuṣṭāva tāṃ brahmā chājñayā paramātmanaḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 11:] He [Kṛṣṇa] said: "Praise that Vāṇī — the sweetest one — O Lord of Creatures!" And Brahmā praised her by the command of the Supreme Self.
contentBlocks.meanings: uvācha = said | sa cha = and he | tāṃ stauhi = praise her | vāṇīm = that Vāṇī (Sarasvatī) | iṣṭāṃ = the most beloved | prajāpatē = O Lord of Creatures (Brahmā) | sa cha = and he | tuṣṭāva tāṃ = praised her | brahmā = Brahmā | cha ājñayā = by the command | paramātmanaḥ = of the Supreme Self

contentBlocks.original:
चकार तत्प्रसादेन तदा सिद्धान्त मुत्तमम्‌ ।
यदाप्यनन्तं पप्रच्छ ज्ञानमेकं वसुन्धरा ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chakāra tatprasādēna tadā siddhānta muttamam‌ ।
yadāpyanantaṃ paprachCha jñānamēkaṃ vasundharā ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 12:] By her grace, [Brahmā] then composed the excellent doctrine. [Second story:] When Ananta (the cosmic serpent) once asked the earth for a single piece of knowledge —
contentBlocks.meanings: chakāra = composed | tat-prasādēna = by her grace | tadā = then | siddhāntam uttamam = the excellent doctrine | yadā api = and when | anantaṃ = Ananta (the thousand-headed serpent bearing the earth) | paprachCha = asked | jñānam ēkaṃ = a single [piece of] knowledge | vasundharā = the earth

contentBlocks.original:
बभूव मूकवत्सोस्पि सिद्धान्तं कर्तु मक्षमः ।
तदा तां च स तुष्टाव सन्त्रस्तः कश्यपाज्ञया ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
babhūva mūkavatsōspi siddhāntaṃ kartu makṣamaḥ ।
tadā tāṃ cha sa tuṣṭāva santrastaḥ kaśyapājñayā ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 13:] [The earth] became like a mute, incapable of resolving the doctrine. Then, frightened, [Ananta] praised [Sarasvatī] by Kaśyapa's instruction.
contentBlocks.meanings: babhūva = became | mūka-vat = like a mute | aspi = even she | siddhāntaṃ = the doctrine | kartum akṣamaḥ = incapable of composing | tadā = then | tāṃ cha = and her | sa tuṣṭāva = he praised | santrastaḥ = frightened | kaśyapa-ājñayā = by Kaśyapa's instruction

contentBlocks.original:
तत श्चकार सिद्धान्तं निर्मलं भ्रम भञ्जनम्‌ ।
व्यासः पुराणसूत्रं च पप्रच्छ वाल्मीकिं यदा ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tata śchakāra siddhāntaṃ nirmalaṃ bhrama bhañjanam‌ ।
vyāsaḥ purāṇasūtraṃ cha paprachCha vālmīkiṃ yadā ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 14:] Then [the earth] composed the pure, error-dispelling doctrine. [Third story:] When Vyāsa once asked Vālmīki about the Purāṇa-sūtra —
contentBlocks.meanings: tataḥ = then | chakāra = composed | siddhāntaṃ = the doctrine | nirmalaṃ = pure | bhrama-bhañjanam = dispelling errors/misconceptions | vyāsaḥ = Vyāsa | purāṇa-sūtraṃ = the Purāṇa-sūtra | cha paprachCha = also asked | vālmīkiṃ = Vālmīki | yadā = when

contentBlocks.original:
मौनीभूत श्च सस्मार तामेव जगदम्बिकाम्‌ ।
तदा चकार सिद्धान्तं तद्वरेण मुनीश्वरः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
maunībhūta ścha sasmāra tāmēva jagadambikām‌ ।
tadā chakāra siddhāntaṃ tadvarēṇa munīśvaraḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 15:] [Vālmīki] fell silent and recalled the very Mother of the World [Sarasvatī]. Then by her boon, the foremost of sages composed the doctrine.
contentBlocks.meanings: maunī-bhūtaḥ = having become silent | cha sasmāra = and recalled | tām ēva = that very [Sarasvatī] | jagad-ambikām = the Mother of the World | tadā = then | chakāra = composed | siddhāntaṃ = the doctrine | tat-varēṇa = by her boon | munīśvaraḥ = the foremost of sages

contentBlocks.original:
सम्प्राप्य निर्मलं ज्ञानं भ्रमान्ध्य ध्वंसदीपकम्‌ ।
पुराणसूत्रं श्रुत्वा च व्यासः कृष्णकलोद्भवः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samprāpya nirmalaṃ jñānaṃ bhramāndhya dhvaṃsadīpakam‌ ।
purāṇasūtraṃ śrutvā cha vyāsaḥ kṛṣṇakalōdbhavaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 16:] Receiving the pure knowledge — the lamp that dispels the blindness of confusion — and hearing the Purāṇa-sūtra, Vyāsa, born of a fraction of Kṛṣṇa —
contentBlocks.meanings: samprāpya = having received | nirmalaṃ jñānaṃ = pure knowledge | bhramāndhya-dhvaṃsa-dīpakam = the lamp that dispels the blindness of confusion | purāṇa-sūtraṃ śrutvā cha = and having heard the Purāṇa-sūtra | vyāsaḥ = Vyāsa | kṛṣṇa-kala-udbhavaḥ = born of a fraction (kalā) of Kṛṣṇa

contentBlocks.original:
तां शिवां वेद दध्यौ च शतवर्षं च पुष्करे ।
तदा त्वत्तो वरं प्राप्य सत्कवीन्द्रो बभूव ह ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāṃ śivāṃ vēda dadhyau cha śatavarṣaṃ cha puṣkarē ।
tadā tvattō varaṃ prāpya satkavīndrō babhūva ha ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 17:] Knew that auspicious Goddess; meditated on her for a hundred years at Puṣkara [lake]. Then having received a boon from you, he became the foremost among good poets.
contentBlocks.meanings: tāṃ śivāṃ = that auspicious Goddess | vēda = knew | dadhyau = meditated | cha = and | śata-varṣaṃ = for a hundred years | puṣkarē = at Puṣkara (the sacred lake in Rājasthān) | tadā tvattō = then from you | varaṃ prāpya = having received a boon | sat-kavīndraḥ = foremost among good poets | babhūva = became | ha = indeed

contentBlocks.original:
तदा वेदविभागं च पुराणं च चकार सः ।
यदा महेन्द्रः पप्रच्छ तत्त्वज्ञानं सदाशिवम्‌ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tadā vēdavibhāgaṃ cha purāṇaṃ cha chakāra saḥ ।
yadā mahēndraḥ paprachCha tattvajñānaṃ sadāśivam‌ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 18:] Then [Vyāsa] composed the division of the Vedas and the Purāṇas. [Fourth story:] When Mahēndra (Indra) once asked Sadāśiva for the knowledge of truth —
contentBlocks.meanings: tadā = then | vēda-vibhāgaṃ = the division of the Vedas | purāṇaṃ = the Purāṇas | cha chakāra = also composed | yadā = when | mahēndraḥ = Mahēndra (Indra) | paprachCha = asked | tattva-jñānaṃ = the knowledge of truth | sadāśivam = Sadāśiva (the eternally auspicious, highest form of Śiva)

contentBlocks.original:
क्षणं तामेव सञ्चिन्त्य तस्मै ज्ञानं ददौ विभुः ।
पप्रच्छ शब्दशास्त्रं च महेन्द्र श्च बृहस्पतिम्‌ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣaṇaṃ tāmēva sañchintya tasmai jñānaṃ dadau vibhuḥ ।
paprachCha śabdaśāstraṃ cha mahēndra ścha bṛhaspatim‌ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 19:] [Sadāśiva] recalled that Goddess for a moment and then gave [Indra] the knowledge. And when Mahēndra asked Bṛhaspati about śabda-śāstra (grammar/linguistics) —
contentBlocks.meanings: kṣaṇaṃ = for a moment | tām ēva = that very [Sarasvatī] | sañchintya = having recalled | tasmai = to him | jñānaṃ dadau = gave knowledge | vibhuḥ = the all-powerful [Sadāśiva] | paprachCha = asked | śabda-śāstraṃ = grammar/the science of language | cha = and | mahēndraḥ = Mahēndra | cha bṛhaspatim = and Bṛhaspati

contentBlocks.original:
दिव्य वर्ष सहस्रं च स त्वां दध्यौ च पुष्करे ।
तदा त्वत्तो वरं प्राप्य दिव्यवर्षसहस्रकम्‌ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
divya varṣa sahasraṃ cha sa tvāṃ dadhyau cha puṣkarē ।
tadā tvattō varaṃ prāpya divyavarṣasahasrakam‌ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 20:] [Bṛhaspati] meditated on you for a thousand divine years at Puṣkara. Then having received a boon from you [for a thousand divine years] —
contentBlocks.meanings: divya-varṣa-sahasraṃ = for a thousand divine years | cha = and | saḥ = he | tvāṃ dadhyau = meditated on you | cha puṣkarē = at Puṣkara lake | tadā = then | tvattō varaṃ prāpya = having received a boon from you | divya-varṣa-sahasrakam = [corresponding to] a thousand divine years

contentBlocks.original:
उवाच शब्द शास्त्रं च तदर्थं च सुरेश्वरम्‌ ।
अध्यापिताश्च ये शिष्या यैरधीतं मुनीश्वरैः ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uvācha śabda śāstraṃ cha tadarthaṃ cha surēśvaram‌ ।
adhyāpitāścha yē śiṣyā yairadhītaṃ munīśvaraiḥ ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 21:] He taught the śabda-śāstra and its meaning to the lord of gods [Indra]. And the disciples who were taught by him, and by whom the great sages learned —
contentBlocks.meanings: uvācha = taught | śabda-śāstraṃ cha = the science of language and | tat-arthaṃ = its meaning | surēśvaraṃ = to the lord of gods (Indra) | adhyāpitāḥ = those taught | cha yē śiṣyāḥ = and those disciples | yaiḥ adhītaṃ = by whom it was learned | munīśvaraiḥ = by the great sages

contentBlocks.original:
ते च तां परिसञ्चित्य प्रवर्तन्ते सुरेश्वरीम्‌ ।
त्वं संस्तुता पूजिता च मुनीन्द्रै र्मनु मानवैः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tē cha tāṃ parisañchitya pravartantē surēśvarīm‌ ।
tvaṃ saṃstutā pūjitā cha munīndrai rmanu mānavaiḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 22:] All of them, meditating on that Goddess, proceed in their work. O Surēśvarī — you are praised and worshipped by the great sages, by Manu, and by all humanity.
contentBlocks.meanings: tē cha = and they | tāṃ parisañchintya = meditating on that Goddess | pravartantē = proceed in their work | surēśvarīm = O Surēśvarī | tvaṃ = you | saṃstutā = are praised | pūjitā cha = and worshipped | munīndraiḥ = by the great sages | manu-mānavaiḥ = by Manu and humanity

contentBlocks.original:
दैत्येन्द्रै श्च सुरैश्चापि ब्रह्म विष्णुशिवादिभिः ।
जडीभूत स्सहस्रास्यः पञ्चवक्त्र श्चतुर्मुखः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daityēndrai ścha suraiśchāpi brahma viṣṇuśivādibhiḥ ।
jaḍībhūta ssahasrāsyaḥ pañchavaktra śchaturmukhaḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 23:] And by the lords of demons, by the gods, by Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Śiva, and others; [before you] even the thousand-mouthed Ananta, the five-faced Śiva, and the four-faced Brahmā become speechless —
contentBlocks.meanings: daityēndraiḥ = by the lords of demons | suraiḥ = by the gods | brahma-viṣṇu-śivādibhiḥ = by Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Śiva, and others | jaḍī-bhūtaḥ = become speechless/paralysed | sahasra-āsyaḥ = thousand-mouthed [Ananta] | pañca-vaktraḥ = five-faced [Śiva] | chatur-mukhaḥ = four-faced [Brahmā]

contentBlocks.original:
यां स्तोतुं कि महं स्तौमि तामेकास्येन मानवः ।
इत्युक्त्वा याज्ञवल्क्य श्च भक्तिनम्रात्म कन्धरः ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāṃ stōtuṃ ki mahaṃ staumi tāmēkāsyēna mānavaḥ ।
ityuktvā yājñavalkya ścha bhaktinamrātma kandharaḥ ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 24:] How can I — a single-mouthed human — praise her whom even these cannot praise? Having said this, Yājñavalkya, with his neck bowed in devotion —
contentBlocks.meanings: yāṃ stōtuṃ = to praise whom | kim = what [can I do] | ahaṃ staumi = I praise | tām ēka-āsyēna = her with a single mouth | mānavaḥ = a human being | ity uktvā = having said this | yājñavalkyaḥ = Yājñavalkya | cha = and | bhakti-namra-ātma-kandhara = with neck bowed in devotion

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणनाम निराहारो रुरोद च मुहुर्मुहुः ।
ज्योतीरूपा महामाया तेन दृष्टा7प्युवाच तम्‌ ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇanāma nirāhārō rurōda cha muhurmuhuḥ ।
jyōtīrūpā mahāmāyā tēna dṛṣṭā7pyuvācha tam‌ ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 25:] Bowed down in prostration, fasting, he wept again and again. The Great Māyā of the form of light, seen by him, spoke to him:
contentBlocks.meanings: praṇanāma = bowed in prostration | nirāhāraḥ = fasting | rurōda = wept | cha muhurmuhuḥ = and again and again | jyōtī-rūpā = of the form of light | mahā-māyā = the Great Māyā (Sarasvatī) | tēna dṛṣṭā = seen by him | api = even so | uvācha = spoke | tam = to him

contentBlocks.original:
सुकवीन्द्रो भवेत्युक्त्वा वैकुण्ठं च जगाम ह ।
याज्ञवल्क्य कृतं वाणी स्तोत्रमेतत्तु यः पठेत्‌ ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sukavīndrō bhavētyuktvā vaikuṇṭhaṃ cha jagāma ha ।
yājñavalkya kṛtaṃ vāṇī stōtramētattu yaḥ paṭhēt‌ ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Verse 26 — Sarasvatī speaks; phalaśruti begins:] "You will become the foremost among good poets!" — having said this, she went to Vaikuṇṭha. Whoever reads this Vāṇī stotram composed by Yājñavalkya —
contentBlocks.meanings: sukavīndraḥ bhava = become the foremost among good poets | ity uktvā = having said this | vaikuṇṭhaṃ = Vaikuṇṭha (the divine abode) | cha jagāma = went | ha = indeed | yājñavalkya-kṛtaṃ = composed by Yājñavalkya | vāṇī-stōtram = this Vāṇī stotram | ētat tu yaḥ paṭhēt = whoever reads this

contentBlocks.original:
स कवीन्द्रो महावाग्मी बृहस्पतिसमो भवेत्‌ ।
महा मूर्खश्च दुर्बुद्धिर्‌-वर्षमेकं यदा पठेत्‌ ।
स पण्डितश्च मेधावी सुकवीन्द्रो भवेद्ध्रुवम्‌ ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa kavīndrō mahāvāgmī bṛhaspatisamō bhavēt‌ ।
mahā mūrkhaścha durbuddhir‌-varṣamēkaṃ yadā paṭhēt‌ ।
sa paṇḍitaścha mēdhāvī sukavīndrō bhavēddhruvam‌ ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti:] Will become the foremost poet, a great orator, equal to Bṛhaspati. Even a great fool of poor understanding, if they read for one year — will certainly become a scholar, mentally sharp, and the foremost among good poets.
contentBlocks.meanings: sa = he | kavīndraḥ = foremost among poets | mahā-vāgmī = great orator | bṛhaspati-samaḥ = equal to Bṛhaspati | bhavēt = will become | mahā-mūrkhaḥ = a great fool | cha durbuddhiḥ = and of poor understanding | varṣam ēkaṃ = for one year | yadā paṭhēt = if reads | sa paṇḍitaḥ = will be a scholar | mēdhāvī = mentally sharp | sukavīndraḥ = foremost among good poets | bhavēt = will become | dhruvam = certainly

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्री देवी भागवते महापुराणे नवमस्कन्धे
सरस्वतीस्तवं नाम पञ्चमोध्यायः ।
सरस्वती कटाक्ष सिद्धिरस्तु ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrī dēvī bhāgavatē mahāpurāṇē navamaskandhē
sarasvatīstavaṃ nāma pañchamōdhyāyaḥ ।
sarasvatī kaṭākṣa siddhirastu ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the fifth chapter named "Sarasvatī-stava" in the ninth skandha of the Śrī Devī Bhāgavata Mahāpurāṇa. May the grace-glance of Sarasvatī be [ours].

contentBlocks.original:
इदं मयाकृतं पारायणं
श्रीसद्गुरु चरणारविन्दार्पणमस्तु ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ mayākṛtaṃ pārāyaṇaṃ
śrīsadguru charaṇāravindārpaṇamastu ।
contentBlocks.translation: May this pārāyaṇa (recitation/reading) performed by me be offered at the lotus feet of the holy sadguru.
